seoTitle: Bonnet Bay vs Bonny Hills — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bonnet Bay and Bonny Hills. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Bonnet Bay vs Bonny Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bonnet Bay and Bonny Hills.
heading: Bonnet Bay vs Bonny Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonnet Bay, New South Wales and Bonny Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonnet Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Bonny Hills (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonnet Bay or Bonny Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonnet Bay has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Bonny Hills scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonnet Bay scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Bonny Hills scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonnet Bay or Bonny Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonnet Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
